steps.title: Explain the protection paradox
steps.content: Three-host CLPs are usually shielded from external conditions during transmission, while directly transmitted parasites are exposed. Even so, three-host CLP abundance decreased as temperatures rose, and directly transmitted parasite abundance stayed largely stable.

steps.title: Account for the decline
steps.content: Choice A compares CLPs with other CLPs in a way the study does not support. Choice B predicts more host species should protect parasites, but the three-host CLPs declined. Choice C falsely says directly transmitted parasites were more shielded. The finding suggests other warming effects outweighed any transmission advantage. The answer is D.
